choices.message: You're annoying. I'm blocking this number.

text: STOP? WHAT DO YOU MEAN, STOP???
I'VE BEEN WRITING TO YOU FOR OVER A YEAR!!!
NEVER A HELLO, NEVER A THANK YOU, NOTHING!!!!
AND I HAVE TO MAKE DO WITH A LOUSY STOP???
DO YOU HAVE ANY IDEA HOW HARD THIS JOB IS???

message: Uh? Is that normal?
Aren't I supposed to get a message saying my request has been received and will be processed within a ridiculously long time?
I didn't ask for anything!
I'm a renter, on top of that!
I couldn't care less about your windows and your solar panels!
Leave me alone

text: Noooooo!!! Wait!!!
I beg you! You have to stay!
I can't take it anymore
I'm going to lose my job if this keeps up!

message: What are you hoping for?
That I'll buy something when I'm broke?
That I'll just let you keep spamming me?
Wow... you're not okay...
Maybe you should see a therapist or a doctor.

messages_in.text: You're right, I think there's nothing left to hope for...
WHAT? Are you calling me crazy?
YOU WANT A CONFIRMATION MESSAGE?? NO PROBLEM, ALLOW ME TO CONFIRM...

I BUST MY BACK FINDING YOU THE BEST PRICES AND THE BEST DEALS ON THINGS THAT ACTUALLY MATTER FOR YOUR LIFE AND ALL I GET IS CONTEMPT!!!
YOU DON'T DESERVE ME!!
YOU DESERVE NOTHING!!!

scenes._notes: No next on choices — the follow-up comes via scene_intro_05 (trigger_after_scene: scene_12). Mom takes time to reply.

messages_in.text: It's mom! Are you coming to stay for a few days next week? Hugs. Mom

choices.message: Why do you always put 'It's Mom' at the start of your messages? I know perfectly well it's you!

messages_in.text: It's mom! I want to make sure you know it's me. I wouldn't want you to get confused. Hugs. Mom

choices.message: Don't worry at all. There's no way I'd ever mix you up with anyone else!

scenes._notes: Opening scene — Maeve tries to make contact

text: It's not spam!
I know it looks like it
But I'm a real person.
Oh!
Thank God...
Do you know where I am?
I don't know
I woke up in this room...
There was this phone on the floor
I've tried other numbers but nobody answers

choices.message: That's exactly what a spammer would say!
I have no idea how I could know where you are.
How long have you been there?

text: I don't have time to try to convince you
I need help
I don't know where I am
I woke up in a room that looks like a laboratory
I have no idea why I'm here!
Yes, it's true.
I'm sorry...
I think this situation is driving me crazy.
I have no idea where I am or why I'm here...
I woke up in a room that looks like a laboratory
There was this phone on the floor.
I woke up here a few hours ago.
A room that looks like a laboratory.
I have absolutely no idea where I am or why I'm here!
I'm going insane...

message: Wow...
This sounds like a bad horror movie...
I'm not sure how I can help
But I'm willing to try.
I'll stay online if that helps.
Ok... Either today is the day of the most reactive and persistent spammers I've ever seen
Or something weird is going on
Either way, I'm starting to want to see where this goes.

text: Thank you...
Really.
It's a bit reassuring to know there's someone on the other side.

messages_in.text: Oh come on, enough with the spam talk!
Well, I'm really glad that the fact I might be in mortal danger is entertaining to you...

message: Don't take it like that!
I just have trouble believing a situation like this could actually happen.
Nothing personal.
I'm willing to give you a chance but...
At the slightest attempt to sell me windows or solar panels, I'm out.
I get a message explaining something completely surreal and I'm supposed to just believe it right away?
If it's true it's serious but I find it hard to believe...
I'll stay online and we'll see.
Honestly, I don't know what to think about any of this.
I want to help but I'm not really sure how right now.
You don't get messages like this every day (and thank god for that!).
There has to be an explanation for this.
Which is why it's important to stay calm and rational
We'll figure it out

text: You're right...
There has to be an explanation!
I'm already grateful you're here.
I understand it seems surreal to you...
But I assure you it's the truth.
I'm a real person and I need help.
Just being here already helps!
I won't lie, I don't really understand what's going on either.
And I don't message random people for help every day.
I know it's hard to believe but I don't really have another choice right now
Do you really think I'd message a stranger if I wasn't desperate?
I'm just asking for a chance to prove I'm telling the truth
No windows or solar panels. I promise.

messages_in.text: Since we're going to be in touch for a while, we might as well know who we're talking to, right?

text: Hmm... {player_name}...
I like that name.

message: Thanks
Maeve is a really nice name too. It's unusual!
Now that we've introduced ourselves, can you tell me what the room looks like?
Ok
So what does the room look like?

messages_in.text: Thanks! It comes from Ireland, my mother is from there. But back to the room...

text: It's a pretty big, very white room
Very 'hospital room' vibes. There's quite a bit of medical equipment, a chair like at the dentist, papers, notepads and screens displaying data I don't understand.
There's also a door but I can't open it...
It's not a regular lock, I think you need a badge or a card to open it.

messages_in.text: Wait, I think the phone I found can take photos. I'll try to take one and send it to you.

message: I think there's a problem with the photo.
I'm not sure the signal is great where you are.

text: I saw yeah...
Hold on...
I'm moving around the room to see if I can find a spot with a better signal.
I think it's better here.

message: Look at that lighting.
Looks cozy.
Send me the Airbnb link for my next vacation!
Yeah...
The perfect place to create or reawaken a trauma!
That's unsettling...

text: :D
No problem, I'll send you the owner's details the minute I find out who it is!
I think that's the first time I've laughed all day! xD
It doesn't help me get out of here but it feels good
PTSD loading

text.text: Please do not interrupt the process.

text: Jokes aside, you understand now why I'm SLIGHTLY stressed about being locked in here?

message: Perfect, it's a deal!
It really feels good to find someone I can count on! :D
If I can't help you get out of there, at least making you laugh is a start!
I know a therapist if you need one!
What's 10 years of therapy anyway?
Do you see anything around you that could help you get out?
I don't really have any ideas...
A hammer? A sheet with a code? ... A tank?

text: Maybe we're witnessing the start of a career change!
Maeve, your Holiday Planner

text.text: Unusual properties for truly awful vacations!

text: Alright...
Would you believe a tank was the first thing I thought of but... no...
They didn't think to leave one in the room!
I'm starting to search everywhere to see if there's a badge or pass that's been lost or left behind.
It would be a bit of a miracle but I did manage to find a phone so you never know...

choices.message: Have you checked under the furniture or in hard-to-reach spots?

message: Do you think it's possible to force the mechanism?
If there are pliers or metal rods, maybe we could try...

text: That was the first thing I checked.
I didn't find anything except some dust balls and stains...
And risk breaking the lock and getting stuck here even longer?
Thanks but no thanks...

choices.message: Have you checked under the furniture or in hard-to-reach spots?

message: Do you think it's possible to force the mechanism?
If there are pliers or metal rods, maybe we could try...

text: That was the first thing I checked.
I didn't find anything except some dust balls and stains...
Did you think I was going to risk breaking the lock and getting stuck here?
Thanks but no thanks...
I'm putting the phone down while I look for something else
I'll message you as soon as I find anything

choices.message: Ok, same here, I'll message you if I think of anything.

message: Maeve?
You okay?

text: The lights!
The power's out!

message: What did you do?
Don't panic
It's probably just a temporary power cut.
You need to stay calm.

text: I'm in complete darkness.
My heart is pounding...
Right now I just want to curl up and cry...
I didn't do anything!
I was looking at a piece of paper and then suddenly nothing...
Complete darkness...
I think I'm on the verge of a heart attack.

message: Stay calm Maeve.
I know it's scary but it's going to be okay.
We'll figure something out!
We need to try something.
The phone you found...
Can't it work as a flashlight?

text: You're right, I need to pull myself together.
The priority is getting out.
I think this phone has a flashlight.
I'll try to turn it on.
I think you're right about the phone.
Hold on, let me try...

choices.message: Ok, I'll keep thinking on my end.

text: Ok, got some light!
It's not very bright but at least I can see.
We're no closer to getting out though...

message: It won't be easy with the lighting situation but we have to keep looking.
I'm thinking about something with the door. The lock is magnetic but if there's no electricity...
The magnet should deactivate, right?

text: Ohh!
Good call!
If that works I'm officially calling you a genius!
Hold on, let me try...
You're right
I need to keep looking.
I just remembered something!
When the power went out, I heard a loud noise and then a second one, more muffled, near the door.
Do you think the power cut could have deactivated the lock mechanism?
Hold on, let me check...

message: That would be insane!
Go look and let me know!

text: The door is open!!
I can finally get out of this room!

message: Amazing!
I'm so happy for you Maeve!!
But this is just the beginning!
Next goal: get out of the building
We'll make it!
